data.contents:
ESLint

We use ESLint as a static code analysis tool for identifying problematic patterns found in JavaScript code.
data.label: ESLint
data.excerpt: We use ESLint as a static code analysis tool for identifying problematic patterns found in JavaScript code.

data.contents:
Express.JS

Express.JS is a minimal and flexible Node.JS web application framework that provides a robust set of features for design and build web applications and APIs. For example, It can be used for simple BFF (backend-for-frontend) implementations logic - targeting simple data aggregations and Auth services. And by doing that, reducing cognitive load on the Frontend Engineers and at the same time leveraging backend teams.
data.label: Express.JS
data.excerpt: Express.JS is a minimal and flexible Node.JS web application framework that provides a robust set of features for design and build web applications and APIs. For example, It can be used for simple BFF (backend-for-frontend) implementations logic - targeting simple data aggregations and Auth services. And by doing that, reducing cognitive load on the Frontend Engineers and at the same time leveraging backend teams.

data.contents:
Jest

Jest is a JavaScript test runner that is designed to ensure the correctness of any JavaScript code-base and gives us integrated code coverage reports, mocking libraries, and parallel test execution. It is the most current used tool for Frontend unit testing and is being used by us and our Business Partners in most Frontend code-bases.
data.label: Jest
data.excerpt: Jest is a JavaScript test runner that is designed to ensure the correctness of any JavaScript code-base and gives us integrated code coverage reports, mocking libraries, and parallel test execution. It is the most current used tool for Frontend unit testing and is being used by us and our Business Partners in most Frontend code-bases.

data.contents:
Babel

Babel is a toolchain that is mainly used to transpile ESNext into a backward-compatible version of JavaScript. We strongly recommend using Babel because it enables us to use the latest features of ECMAScript in the browser.
data.label: Babel
data.excerpt: Babel is a toolchain that is mainly used to transpile ESNext into a backward-compatible version of JavaScript. We strongly recommend using Babel because it enables us to use the latest features of ECMAScript in the browser.

data.contents:
HERE Maps

HERE Maps is a web mapping navigation service that is currently being used by our Business partners and we support it.
data.label: HERE Maps
data.excerpt: HERE Maps is a web mapping navigation service that is currently being used by our Business partners and we support it.

data.contents:
NgRx

NgRx provides reactive state management for Angular by following redux patterns. It is widely used by some of our Business Partners and therefore we support it.
data.label: NgRx
data.excerpt: NgRx provides reactive state management for Angular by following redux patterns. It is widely used by some of our Business Partners and therefore we support it.

data.contents:
Angular

Angular is the biggest and most-known Frontend framework used in Enterprise applications. We support it because It is been widely used by some of our Business Partners. However, we prefer using React over Angular since it helps us achieve better stability and control in our applications.
data.label: Angular
data.excerpt: Angular is the biggest and most-known Frontend framework used in Enterprise applications. We support it because It is been widely used by some of our Business Partners. However, we prefer using React over Angular since it helps us achieve better stability and control in our applications.

data.contents:
NGINX

NGINX is a high‑performance and scalable web server, reverse proxy server, and web accelerator for combining the features of an HTTP load balancer, content cache, and more.
data.label: NGINX
data.excerpt: NGINX is a high‑performance and scalable web server, reverse proxy server, and web accelerator for combining the features of an HTTP load balancer, content cache, and more.

data.contents:
Google Maps

Google Maps is a web mapping tool that we support since It is currently being used by some Business partners.
data.label: Google Maps
data.excerpt: Google Maps is a web mapping tool that we support since It is currently being used by some Business partners.

data.contents:
Next.JS

Next.JS provides an abstraction on top of React tooling - Convention over configuration. We should assess it for the development of POCs and internal tools plus improving time-to-market.
data.label: Next.JS
data.excerpt: Next.JS provides an abstraction on top of React tooling - Convention over configuration. We should assess it for the development of POCs and internal tools plus improving time-to-market.

data.contents:
Mapbox

Mapbox is a location data platform that powers the maps and location services. Given the lack of usage by our Business Partners, we do not support it at the moment.
data.label: Mapbox
data.excerpt: Mapbox is a location data platform that powers the maps and location services. Given the lack of usage by our Business Partners, we do not support it at the moment.

data.contents:
npm

Node Package Manager is the default package manager and since Node.JS is heavily dependant on NPM, we have to support it.
data.label: npm
data.excerpt: Node Package Manager is the default package manager and since Node.JS is heavily dependant on NPM, we have to support it.

data.contents:
Node.JS

Node.JS is an open-source, cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment that runs on the V8 engine and executes JavaScript code outside a web browser. We successfully use it since It’s a good choice for restful APIs, real-time purposes/situations where many concurrent connections are expected.
data.label: Node.JS
data.excerpt: Node.JS is an open-source, cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment that runs on the V8 engine and executes JavaScript code outside a web browser. We successfully use it since It’s a good choice for restful APIs, real-time purposes/situations where many concurrent connections are expected.

data.contents:
Prettier

We use Prettier to have a common code style guide that is valuable for our projects and teams.
data.label: Prettier
data.excerpt: We use Prettier to have a common code style guide that is valuable for our projects and teams.

data.contents:
React.JS

React.JS makes it painless to create interactive UIs and we see great potential to build upcoming projects with React. It follows functional programming paradigms like immutability, unidirectional data flow, and function composition that provide better code readability, fewer errors, and higher confidence in the Application life cycle which leads to saving overall costs in development and production.
data.label: React.JS
data.excerpt: React.JS makes it painless to create interactive UIs and we see great potential to build upcoming projects with React. It follows functional programming paradigms like immutability, unidirectional data flow, and function composition that provide better code readability, fewer errors, and higher confidence in the Application life cycle which leads to saving overall costs in development and production.

data.contents:
Storybook

Storybook provides documentation and component readability to improve the developer experience via developing UI components in isolation.
data.label: Storybook
data.excerpt: Storybook provides documentation and component readability to improve the developer experience via developing UI components in isolation.

data.contents:
Redux

Redux helps us to maintain the state of our Frontend applications in a more predictable and clearer way by following the Flux pattern and functional programming concepts like immutability and unidirectional data flows which are highly relevant to avoid errors in large-scale stateful applications. Currently, we use Redux only in our React projects.
data.label: Redux
data.excerpt: Redux helps us to maintain the state of our Frontend applications in a more predictable and clearer way by following the Flux pattern and functional programming concepts like immutability and unidirectional data flows which are highly relevant to avoid errors in large-scale stateful applications. Currently, we use Redux only in our React projects.

data.contents:
Styled-components

Styled-components is our go-to solution for React code-bases. With some solid production experience under our belts, we can now adopt it since it is the solution to enhance CSS for styling React component systems, by providing a better Developer Experience and following methodologies of Isolation, scoping, and atomic CSS.
data.label: Styled-components
data.excerpt: Styled-components is our go-to solution for React code-bases. With some solid production experience under our belts, we can now adopt it since it is the solution to enhance CSS for styling React component systems, by providing a better Developer Experience and following methodologies of Isolation, scoping, and atomic CSS.

data.contents:
Sass

Sass is a preprocessor scripting language and a powerful professional-grade CSS extension that is interpreted/compiled into CSS. It was created to respond to developer needs that were not supported by native CSS, at the time. Although more modern and better alternatives are out there (mainly targeting atomic CSS), since Sass is widely used within our Business Partners code-bases, we have to support it.
data.label: Sass
data.excerpt: Sass is a preprocessor scripting language and a powerful professional-grade CSS extension that is interpreted/compiled into CSS. It was created to respond to developer needs that were not supported by native CSS, at the time. Although more modern and better alternatives are out there (mainly targeting atomic CSS), since Sass is widely used within our Business Partners code-bases, we have to support it.

data.contents:
TypeScript

TypeScript Is an open-source language, created by Microsoft to respond to the challenges of dealing with large applications built on complex JavaScript code-base. It provides highly productive development tools and practices by adding static type definitions and since it compiles to JavaScript, it can make use of most of its features in the browser, and in the server through Node.js or Deno.
data.label: TypeScript
data.excerpt: TypeScript Is an open-source language, created by Microsoft to respond to the challenges of dealing with large applications built on complex JavaScript code-base. It provides highly productive development tools and practices by adding static type definitions and since it compiles to JavaScript, it can make use of most of its features in the browser, and in the server through Node.js or Deno.

data.contents:
Testing Library

We use Testing Library as a solution for testing React components. It provides light utility functions on top of react-dom in a way that encourages better testing practices.
data.label: Testing Library
data.excerpt: We use Testing Library as a solution for testing React components. It provides light utility functions on top of react-dom in a way that encourages better testing practices.

data.contents:
Webpack

Webpack is the standard module bundler for most modern frontend frameworks. We have adopted Webpack for nearly every SPA because it gives us great benefits.
data.label: Webpack
data.excerpt: Webpack is the standard module bundler for most modern frontend frameworks. We have adopted Webpack for nearly every SPA because it gives us great benefits.

data.contents:
Yarn

Yarn is a fast, reliable, and secure dependency management and It is tb.lx Frontend tool of choice.
data.label: Yarn
data.excerpt: Yarn is a fast, reliable, and secure dependency management and It is tb.lx Frontend tool of choice.
